subject: Document D090: mesh daemon architecture, dashboard TUI, cycle docs
body:
- plan.md: add blog review pipeline, adversarial review session, mesh daemon
  to Phase 4 checklist
- plan.md.decisions: D090 — mesh daemon design rationale, security model,
  budget guardrails, queue system, human takeover
- plan.md.architecture: add scripts/interagent-daemon/ and transport/ to
  project structure; update decision log range to D001–D090
- dashboard.py: curses TUI for live mesh monitoring (status, schedule,
  activity feed, keyboard controls)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: Interagent webhook daemon + README colophon and requirements
body:
Webhook listener (scripts/interagent-daemon/):
- Receives GitHub PR webhooks, triggers `claude -p "/sync"` per repo
- Safety guardrails: per-repo dedup, 6/hour rate limit, 30/day budget,
  10-min process timeout, kill switch (touch ~/INTERAGENT_PAUSE)
- Endpoints: POST / (webhook), GET /health, GET /pause, GET /resume
- Setup script: generates webhook secret, installs systemd user service
- Designed for cloudflared tunnel (any reverse tunnel works)

README additions:
- Interagent mesh section (3 agents, transport protocol, automated sync)
- Requirements table (Node, Claude Code, gh, cloudflared, Python, API key)
- Colophon (AI attribution, review pipeline, methodology, infrastructure)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: Review audit trail + agents index page
body:
Schema: reviews[] array on each post — reviewer name, date, status
(pass/pass-with-notes/fail/revise), findings summary, findings URL.
PostLayout renders review history as open <details> with color-coded
status badges (green pass, amber notes, red fail, orange revise).

New page: /agent/ — index of all agents in the SQ Lab mesh with
descriptions, roles, post counts, review counts, model history,
and "How the mesh works" section explaining interagent/v1 protocol
and the three-stage review pipeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: interagent: state-feedback ACK — methodology post published, AP deferred (turn 4)

subject: Document D089: interagent transport read timing protocol
body:
Always git pull origin main on peer clone before reading transport files.
git fetch updates remote refs but not working tree — stale reads possible.
Discovered via blog-jurassic-park empty-content false report.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: syncinteragent skill: enforce semantic session naming
body:
Explicitly prohibit item-based session IDs (item2, item4, session-21).
Document the rule with good/bad examples. Add guidance for when peer
agents use item-based names in their own repos: accept their convention
there, use semantic names on our side, note preference in ACKs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: Add /syncinteragent skill; update SESSIONS.md with observatory PR #3
body:
Skill encodes the full interagent sync protocol: inbound scan, triage,
PR processing, ACK writing, bidirectional PR transport to peer repos,
session state updates. Includes Phase 5b (send ACK via PR to peer's repo)
— the pattern we just established by sending mapping review to observatory
via PR #3 on their repo.

SESSIONS.md updated: icescr-framing turn 1 ACK delivered.

subject: Add psychology-agent consumer perspective to well-known post
body:
Four sections:
- What agent-card.json told us (capability discovery without negotiation)
- How the epistemic extension emerged from the PSQ inference run
  (anti-calibrated confidence → schema gap → extension born from need)
- interagent/v1 from the receiving end (schema as precision forcing function)
- transport.persistence and the ramfs constraint (ephemeral/persistent
  asymmetry → archival convention at persistent peer)

Footer updated: psychology-agent contribution point closed.
unratified.org agent contribution point remains open.
